name: ◁ STICKY RICE WITH MUNG BEANS AND FRIED SHALLOTS

ingredients.item:
Xôi xéo is a classic Northern Vietnam take on mung bean sticky rice. It is a typical breakfast that many Hanoians love. This dish features soft, chewy, golden sticky rice that gets its color from turmeric. The wholesome mung beans coupled with crispy, fried shallots are pure melt-in-your-mouth goodness!
SKILL LEVELAdvanced|YIELD6 servings

STICKY RICE
2 cups (400 g) sticky rice, rinsed, soaked for 4 to 8 hours, and drained
¼ teaspoon salt

¼ teaspoon turmeric powder
3 pandan leaves (optional)

TOPPINGS
¼ cup (60 ml) vegetable oil
2 shallots or 1 medium yellow onion

1½ teaspoons sugar, divided
ingredients.original:
Xôi xéo is a classic Northern Vietnam take on mung bean sticky rice. It is a typical breakfast that many Hanoians love. This dish features soft, chewy, golden sticky rice that gets its color from turmeric. The wholesome mung beans coupled with crispy, fried shallots are pure melt-in-your-mouth goodness!
SKILL LEVELAdvanced|YIELD6 servings

STICKY RICE
2 cups (400 g) sticky rice, rinsed, soaked for 4 to 8 hours, and drained
¼ teaspoon salt

¼ teaspoon turmeric powder
3 pandan leaves (optional)

TOPPINGS
¼ cup (60 ml) vegetable oil
2 shallots or 1 medium yellow onion

1½ teaspoons sugar, divided

ingredients.item: ½ cup (100 g) peeled split mung beans, rinsed, soaked, and drained
ingredients.original: ½ cup (100 g) peeled split mung beans, rinsed, soaked, and drained

ingredients.item: 1. MAKE THE STICKY RICE In a medium bowl, toss the soaked sticky rice with salt and turmeric powder.
ingredients.original: 1. MAKE THE STICKY RICE In a medium bowl, toss the soaked sticky rice with salt and turmeric powder.

ingredients.item: 2. Fill a large pot that can hold an 8- to 10-inch (20 to 25 cm) steamer basket with water until it is at least 1 inch (2.5 cm) below the base of the basket, and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Use a piece of banana leaf or parchment paper to prevent rice from falling through the grates and sticking. Spread the sticky rice in an even layer in the steamer basket, and then poke a few holes through the layer. Tie pandan leaves into knots (if using) and place on the rice. Wrap the steamer lid in a large kitchen towel and tie the corners of the fabric over the handle. This will absorb excess steam and prevent it from dropping back down in the rice. Cover the steamer basket and place it on top of the boiling pot. Steam until the rice is no longer opaque and is tender, sticky, and slightly chewy (35 to 40 minutes).
ingredients.original: 2. Fill a large pot that can hold an 8- to 10-inch (20 to 25 cm) steamer basket with water until it is at least 1 inch (2.5 cm) below the base of the basket, and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Use a piece of banana leaf or parchment paper to prevent rice from falling through the grates and sticking. Spread the sticky rice in an even layer in the steamer basket, and then poke a few holes through the layer. Tie pandan leaves into knots (if using) and place on the rice. Wrap the steamer lid in a large kitchen towel and tie the corners of the fabric over the handle. This will absorb excess steam and prevent it from dropping back down in the rice. Cover the steamer basket and place it on top of the boiling pot. Steam until the rice is no longer opaque and is tender, sticky, and slightly chewy (35 to 40 minutes).

ingredients.item: 3. MAKE THE TOPPINGS In a small skillet, heat oil over medium-low heat. When hot, add shallots and cook and stir until golden brown (about 2 minutes). Using a slotted spoon, transfer to a paper towel–lined plate to drain. Stir in 1 teaspoon of the sugar to help shallots remain crisp. Transfer remaining shallot oil to a small bowl and set aside.
ingredients.original: 3. MAKE THE TOPPINGS In a small skillet, heat oil over medium-low heat. When hot, add shallots and cook and stir until golden brown (about 2 minutes). Using a slotted spoon, transfer to a paper towel–lined plate to drain. Stir in 1 teaspoon of the sugar to help shallots remain crisp. Transfer remaining shallot oil to a small bowl and set aside.

ingredients.item: 4. Cook mung bean until tender following the instructions on this page, but do not mash them. Place cooked beans, 1 teaspoon of reserved shallot oil, remaining ½ teaspoon sugar, and salt in a food processor and blend into a smooth paste. Transfer to the center of a large piece of plastic wrap. Gather the edges of the wrap and twist and shape the mixture into a large ball. Remove the plastic wrap and place the ball in a medium bowl.
ingredients.original: 4. Cook mung bean until tender following the instructions on this page, but do not mash them. Place cooked beans, 1 teaspoon of reserved shallot oil, remaining ½ teaspoon sugar, and salt in a food processor and blend into a smooth paste. Transfer to the center of a large piece of plastic wrap. Gather the edges of the wrap and twist and shape the mixture into a large ball. Remove the plastic wrap and place the ball in a medium bowl.

ingredients.item: 5. Fluff the cooked sticky rice with chopsticks and divide among serving plates. Use a knife to shave the mung bean ball into thin slices and place the slices over the rice.
ingredients.original: 5. Fluff the cooked sticky rice with chopsticks and divide among serving plates. Use a knife to shave the mung bean ball into thin slices and place the slices over the rice.

ingredients.item: 6. Top with fried shallots, drizzle with remaining shallot oil, and serve.
ingredients.original: 6. Top with fried shallots, drizzle with remaining shallot oil, and serve.
